methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $22M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 9 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Critical Energy, a seed-stage climate and energy company founded by Spencer Jackson, has raised $22M to date. The valuation universe for climate and energy is populated by 9 private peers (including Helion, Redwood Materials, QuantumScape, KoBold Metals, and Electric Hydrogen) and 1 public comparable (LNZA), though a narrow peer set of same-stage or adjacent-stage companies meeting tight valuation-tier filters could not be constructed with sufficient density to support a reliable median-based approach.

Stage-progression methodology, applying a 2.5x step-up multiplier from seed to the next stage against the $22M capital raised, yields an implied valuation of $55M. This method remains the only quantitatively applicable triangulation approach given the peer-set constraints. No peer-median calculation could be executed, as the broader Climate & Energy sector yielded fewer than three datapoints within relevant stage and tier bands.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $47M to $63M, centered on a $55M median derived solely from stage-progression mechanics. Key uncertainties include the absence of direct peer benchmarking at the same stage and tier, which leaves the implied step-up multiplier unvalidated by comparable transaction patterns. Investors should note that this range reflects capital-deployed scaling convention rather than market-derived pricing signals, and should be stress-tested against sector-specific unit economics and competitive positioning as those become available.
